core_mechanism: an information-bottleneck compression principle — constraining a system to a minimal low-dimensional representation that retains only the information predictive of future states, i.e. a rate-distortion trade-off between compressing the input and preserving predictive information, with an information-theoretic order parameter that moves non-monotonically (dip then peak) across the memorization-to-generalization transition and whose magnitude predicts out-of-distribution performance
brief_reason: The abstract states that an explicit information bottleneck forcing a low-dimensional representation is necessary for out-of-distribution generalization, and that an information-theoretic causal-emergence measure follows a non-monotonic trajectory (initial decrease, minimum, rise to maximum) whose magnitude reliably predicts generalization.
